question.text: Anand, a Hindu male, dies without making a will. Under which section of the Indian Succession Act is this situation defined, and what does it mean?

answer.text: Under Sec 2(h), it means dying with a registered will that is later found invalid
Under Sec 30, dying intestate means dying without any testamentary disposition of property
Under Sec 59, it means dying after making an oral will before two witnesses
Under Sec 18, it means dying without registering the will with the sub-registrar

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 30 of the Indian Succession Act, dying intestate means dying without making any testamentary disposition of property. For Hindus dying intestate, the Hindu Succession Act 1956 governs distribution. **The Trap:** Sec 2(h) defines a "will" not intestacy. Sec 59 deals with testamentary capacity. Sec 18 deals with registration. Candidates who mix up section numbers will fall for these distractors. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-B specifically tests succession law sections. Understanding section numbers is critical for this exam. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In India, over 80% of people die intestate. Always urge clients to make a will — it is the single most impactful estate planning action.
